text.title: Risk Register Framework
text.summary: Six-step cycle for identifying, scoring, responding to, and monitoring project risks and opportunities.

section.title: Identification
section.p: Brainstorm threats and opportunities across four categories: Technical (technology failures, complexity), External (vendor delays, regulation), Organizational (resource loss, priority shifts), Project Management (poor estimates, scope creep). Techniques: team brainstorming, expert interviews, checklist review, historical project analysis.

list.item: Very Low — <5% budget or schedule effect

section.title: Scoring
section.p: Risk Score = Probability level (1-5) x Impact level (1-5). Priority: 15-25 = High (immediate action); 8-14 = Medium (monitor closely); 1-7 = Low (periodic review).

list.item: Avoid — eliminate the threat by changing approach or removing the risky element
Transfer — shift to a third party via insurance, outsourcing, or contractual clauses
Mitigate — reduce probability or impact (prototype first, add testing, cross-train)
Accept — acknowledge and plan contingency; requires contingency_amount or contingency_plan
Exploit — ensure the opportunity occurs (assign best resources, accelerate)
Share — partner with another party to capture the benefit
Enhance — increase probability or impact (more marketing, better timing)
Accept — take the benefit if it occurs; no special action required

section.title: Ownership and Monitoring
section.p: Every risk has exactly one named owner responsible for: monitoring triggers, executing the response if the trigger fires, and updating status. Review weekly: add new risks, update P/I as situation changes, close resolved risks, track response effectiveness. Top-5 risks appear at every steering committee.
